world.title: The US Was Right: Iran Has A 'Nuclear' Weapon, But It's Not A Missile
world.summary: The U.S. asserts that while Iran does not possess a nuclear weapon, it has significant strategic leverage by controlling the Strait of Hormuz, a crucial oil and gas shipping route. Iran reportedly has around 440 kg of 60% enriched uranium, which is below the 90% threshold for weaponization but could potentially be used to create multiple nuclear bombs.

world.title: US military intercepts sanctioned M/T Tifani ship linked to Iranian smuggling
world.summary: The US military intercepted the stateless M/T Tifani, linked to Iranian smuggling, as part of efforts to disrupt illicit maritime networks. The Pentagon confirmed the boarding took place during overnight hours under the jurisdiction of US INDOPACOM. This action is part of broader global maritime enforcement initiatives aimed at addressing sanctioned activities and enhancing security in international waters.

world.title: Disturbing video surfaces as Klaudia Zakrzewska hit by Gabrielle Carrington Mercedes after clash outside London nightclub
world.summary: Klaudia Zakrzewska, known as Klaudia Glam, is in critical condition following a collision outside a London nightclub early Sunday. A video has emerged showing the incident involving Gabrielle Carrington's Mercedes. The clash occurred after an altercation, leading to Zakrzewska being struck by the vehicle. Authorities are investigating the circumstances surrounding the incident and its aftermath.

business.title: Tim Cook called to 'kiss my a**': Trump's post on Apple CEO stepping down takes odd turn
business.summary: Donald Trump praised Tim Cook as an “incredible guy” and expressed his admiration for him following Apple’s announcement that Cook will transition to executive chairman and step down as CEO. In a post on Truth Social, Trump referred to himself as a “big fan” of Cook, highlighting a positive relationship despite previous tensions between Trump and Apple.

business.title: NSE IPO DRHP filing moves closer! SEBI clears Rs 1,800 crore settlement path
business.summary: The National Stock Exchange (NSE) is closer to filing its Draft Red Herring Prospectus (DRHP) for an initial public offering (IPO) following a SEBI panel's approval of a settlement fee of approximately Rs 1,800 crore. This settlement pertains to ongoing investigations related to co-location and dark fibre cases, facilitating the NSE's IPO process.

business.title: India should aim to achieve 100% ethanol blending: Nitin Gadkari
business.summary: Road Transport and Highways Minister Nitin Gadkari emphasized that India should aim for 100% ethanol blending to enhance energy self-reliance. He highlighted the current energy crisis stemming from the West Asia conflict, underscoring the need for the country to reduce dependence on oil imports. Achieving this goal is seen as crucial for ensuring energy security in India.

technology.title: gpt-image-2-OpenAI-Artificial intellligence-ChatGPT-AI - Technology
technology.summary: OpenAI's upcoming tool, gpt-image-2, aims to transform AI image generation, making it challenging to differentiate between real and AI-created images. This advancement is expected to enhance the quality and realism of generated visuals significantly. Prabhat Shukla, a Senior Copy Editor at ET Now, has over 2.5 years of experience in reporting and holds degrees in Economics and Journalism.

technology.title: 5 Docker Best Practices for Faster Builds and Smaller Images
technology.summary: Implementing effective Docker practices can enhance build speed and reduce image size. Key strategies include minimizing the number of layers, using multi-stage builds, optimizing caching, and cleaning up unnecessary files. These methods ensure images remain compact and production-ready, ultimately improving deployment efficiency. By following these best practices, developers can streamline their workflows and enhance overall performance in container management.

technology.title: McDonald's MangoBurst is back for its third year and at this point, it's not a trend - it's a tradition | McDonald's India West & South
technology.summary: McDonald's India has reintroduced the MangoBurst McSwirl and McFlurry with Oreo for a limited time, marking its third consecutive year. The MangoBurst McSwirl features a creamy mango dip encased in a delicate shell, offering a satisfying dessert experience with a contrast of smoothness and crunch. This seasonal offering has become a tradition for customers during summer.

sports.title: "He And Ajinkya Rahane Are Fighting For Worst Batter's Tag": World Cup Winner Kris Srikkanth Shows No Mercy On Two IPL Captains
sports.summary: Krishnamachari Srikkanth has been vocal in his critiques during the IPL 2026 season, targeting players' performances. He specifically criticized Rajasthan Royals captain Sanju Samson and Chennai Super Kings captain Ajinkya Rahane, suggesting they are competing for the title of worst batter. Srikkanth's comments reflect his straightforward approach to analyzing the game and the players' contributions this season.

sports.title: Marcus Rashford & Frenkie de Jong among FIVE Barcelona stars set to be moved on in summer transfer window
sports.summary: Barcelona plans a major squad overhaul this summer, potentially moving on five key players, including Marcus Rashford and Frenkie de Jong. Hansi Flick and the Barcelona board have identified these players as having "completed a cycle." The list reportedly includes high earners and those with expiring contracts, as the club aims to restructure its team for future success.

sports.title: ‘Tera Promotion Hua? Commissioner…’: Bumrah Complains DSP Siraj Doesn’t Send Police Vehicle | Watch
sports.summary: Tilak Varma scored his first IPL century as the Mumbai Indians defeated the Gujarat Titans by 99 runs in match 30 of IPL 2026. Following the match, Jasprit Bumrah and DSP Siraj engaged in playful banter about biryani and Siraj's role as a Deputy Superintendent of Police. The game showcased strong performances from the Mumbai team.

health.title: Weight loss drugs: Why experts worry about muscle loss instead of just fat
health.summary: Weight loss drugs are increasingly popular, raising concerns about potential muscle loss alongside fat reduction. Experts emphasize the importance of understanding how these medications affect body composition and long-term metabolic health. Factors such as diet, protein intake, and resistance training play crucial roles in preserving muscle mass during weight loss. Consulting health practitioners before starting any regimen is advised.

health.title: CARE Hospitals Launches Headache Clinic in Visakhapatnam for Early Diagnosis
health.summary: CARE Hospitals in Visakhapatnam has launched a specialized headache clinic to enhance early diagnosis and evidence-based treatment for chronic and recurrent headache disorders. This initiative addresses the increasing prevalence of headache disorders, which are frequently underdiagnosed and often managed by patients themselves. The clinic aims to provide targeted care and improve overall management of these conditions.

health.title: Addressing Discrimination and False Cures in Type 1 Diabetes: What we can learn from HIV Rights-based Response
health.summary: India has a significant number of children with Type 1 diabetes, facing stigma and discrimination. The health ministry's efforts to provide equitable treatment access are lacking. The first Global Summit to End Diabetes Stigma highlighted the potential of applying human rights law to combat discrimination in healthcare, education, and employment, drawing parallels with the HIV/AIDS response for legal reform.

science.title: Microbial Survival Under Mars Like Conditions
science.summary: Scientists conducted experiments to simulate Mars-like conditions in a laboratory to investigate microbial survival. The study, published in PNAS Nexus, aimed to determine if life could endure extreme environments similar to those found on Mars. Results indicated that certain microbes exhibited resilience, suggesting potential for life in extraterrestrial settings. The findings contribute to understanding life's adaptability in harsh conditions.

science.title: Chinese astronomers discover pulsar that should not exist
science.summary: Chinese astronomers have identified a millisecond pulsar exhibiting an unusually high spin-down rate, strong magnetic field, and significant energy loss. This discovery challenges existing theories, as such a pulsar is not expected to form under conventional astrophysical models. The findings may prompt a reevaluation of current understanding regarding pulsar formation and characteristics in the field of astronomy.

science.title: Two-sun planets keep disappearing: Here’s why scientists point to Einstein
science.summary: Astronomers have confirmed the existence of circumbinary planets, which orbit two suns. However, many of these planets are disappearing, prompting scientists to investigate the phenomenon. They point to Einstein's theories as a potential explanation for the loss of these celestial bodies. The concept of planets with dual suns has intrigued both scientists and the public, often featured in science fiction.

entertainment.title: Ranveer Singh starrer ‘Dhurandhar’ BTS documentary to release as a full feature film in theatres by late 2026 - Reports
entertainment.summary: The making-of documentary for the film 'Dhurandhar,' featuring Ranveer Singh and Arjun Rampal, is set to be released as a full feature film in theaters by late 2026. This follows the success of the 'Dhurandhar' spy-thriller duology, which achieved significant box office records, with the sequel earning ₹1,100 crore domestically.

entertainment.title: JD Chakravarthy Exposes Power Abuse Against Heroines in TFI
entertainment.summary: Tollywood actor JD Chakravarthy has ignited a significant discussion regarding power abuse and exploitation in the film industry. He recounted a troubling incident from his early career, highlighting the pervasive issue of the casting couch that many actresses encounter. His remarks have prompted renewed scrutiny of the challenges faced by heroines in Tollywood, emphasizing the need for change.

entertainment.title: Jailer 2: Vasanth Ravi’s presence at the wrap party raises questions
entertainment.summary: A recent photo from the wrap party of Rajinikanth’s Jailer 2 has sparked curiosity among fans due to the presence of Vasanth Ravi. His character was killed in the climax of the first film, leading to speculation about his role in the sequel. The image has generated discussions regarding potential plot developments in the upcoming movie.
